You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_10.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 10: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[10]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_10.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 10: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-beatboth-011 #4 + #7 (#1289) — behavioral replay harness for the weighted-
3	enrichment sanitize + same-work consolidate fix.
4	
5	§-1.4 acceptance gate: this proves the EFFECT FIRES in the REAL output of the REAL
6	``build_verified_span_draft`` function (not a reimplementation, not a config check):
7	
8	  (a) #4 RENDER SAFETY: a span carrying a literal NUL byte + a ~75K raw-metadata blob,
9	      after the real draft build, emits ZERO NUL/C0 control bytes and the oversized
10	      blob is screened/bounded out (never reaches the render).
11	  (b) #7 SAME-WORK CONSOLIDATION: 4 members that are the SAME WORK (same DOI,
12	      different URLs) collapse to ONE multi-citation unit-set that STILL retains all 4
13	      URLs as grounding corroborator markers (multi-URL corroboration, ONE source).
14	  (b2) #4 OVER-MERGE NEGATIVE CONTROL: two records with the SAME folded title but
15	      DIFFERENT year AND different first author (no DOI) are DIFFERENT works and must
16	      NOT consolidate — each surfaces as its OWN distinct single-marker unit.
17	  (c) #7 CAPTCHA DROP: a member whose fetched text is a CAPTCHA / security stub
18	      ("Just a moment... Performing security verification") is dropped entirely.
19	
20	FAIL LOUD: sys.exit(1) with a diagnostic on ANY regression; sys.exit(0) only if all
21	three checks pass. No network, no LLM, no faithfulness-gate touch — pure offline replay.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import os
27	import re
28	import sys
29	
30	# Ensure repo root on path when run from anywhere.
31	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
32	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:
33	    sys.path.insert(0, _REPO_ROOT)
34	
35	from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: E402
36	    build_verified_span_draft,
37	)
38	
39	# Legacy [ev_id] marker form the production rewriter consumes (live_deepseek_generator.py:64).
40	_EV_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\]")
41	
42	_FAILURES: list[str] = []
43	
44	
45	def _fail(check: str, msg: str) -> None:
46	    _FAILURES.append(f"[{check}] {msg}")
47	
48	
49	# A clean clinical claim sentence used as the shared verbatim quote for the same-work
50	# group. Long enough to clear _MIN_UNIT_CHARS (40) and short enough to clear the render
51	# char bound (default 600).
52	_CLEAN_SENTENCE = (
53	    "Automation displaced roughly 12 percent of routine clerical tasks across the "
54	    "surveyed labor markets between 2020 and 2024"
55	)
56	
57	
58	def _check_a_render_safety() -> None:
59	    """#4: NUL + 75K blob span must never reach the render."""
60	    blob = "x9 = sum over j of qj " * 4000  # ~88K chars of raw-extraction salad
61	    assert len(blob) > 75_000, "test blob must exceed 75K chars"
62	    # The clean sentence ITSELF carries an embedded NUL + C0/DEL bytes, so this check
63	    # exercises the control-byte STRIP on an IN-BOUND, surviving unit (not only the
64	    # char-bound drop of the blob) — the strip is independently load-bearing.
65	    clean_with_ctrl = (
66	        "Automation displaced roughly 12\x00 percent of routine\x01 clerical tasks "
67	        "across the surveyed\x7f labor markets between 2020 and 2024"
68	    )
69	    poisoned_quote = (
70	        f"{clean_with_ctrl}.\nKD = sum_j Rk \x00 wj(h) \x00 Rk equilibrium dump "
71	        f"{blob}\x01\x02end."
72	    )
73	    pool = {
74	        "ev_poison": {
75	            "evidence_id": "ev_poison",
76	            "direct_quote": poisoned_quote,
77	            "doi": "10.1234/poison",
78	            "title": "Equilibrium properties of automated labor markets",
79	            "source_url": "https://example.org/poison",
80	        },
81	    }
82	    draft = build_verified_span_draft(["ev_poison"], pool)
83	
84	    if "\x00" in draft:
85	        _fail("a", "draft STILL contains a NUL byte (\\x00) — binary corruption not fixed")
86	    for cp in range(0x20):
87	        ch = chr(cp)
88	        if ch in ("\n", "\t"):
89	            continue
90	        if ch in draft:
91	            _fail("a", f"draft contains C0 control byte U+{cp:04X} — control bytes not stripped")
92	    if "\x7f" in draft:
93	        _fail("a", "draft contains DEL (0x7F) — control bytes not stripped")
94	    # The clean sentence (under the bound) SHOULD still surface; the blob must NOT.
95	    if "Automation displaced roughly 12 percent" not in draft:
96	        _fail("a", "the clean in-bound clinical sentence was lost (over-screening)")
97	    if "sum over j of qj" in draft or len(draft) > 5_000:
98	        _fail(
99	            "a",
100	            f"the oversized raw-extraction blob leaked into the render (draft_chars={len(draft)})",
101	        )
102	
103	
104	def _check_b_same_work_consolidation() -> None:
105	    """#7: 4 same-work members (same DOI, 4 URLs) collapse to ONE multi-citation unit."""
106	    quote = f"{_CLEAN_SENTENCE}."
107	    shared_doi = "10.1000/labor.2024.001"
108	    member_ids = ["ev_w1", "ev_w2", "ev_w3", "ev_w4"]
109	    urls = [
110	        "https://journal.example.org/full",
111	        "https://mirror.example.net/pdf",
112	        "https://repo.example.edu/preprint",
113	        "https://aggregator.example.com/view",
114	    ]
115	    pool = {
116	        eid: {
117	            "evidence_id": eid,
118	            # SAME verbatim quote on each fetch (multi-URL corroboration of one work).
119	            "direct_quote": quote,
120	            "doi": shared_doi,
121	            "title": "Automation and the clerical labor market",
122	            "source_url": url,
123	        }
124	        for eid, url in zip(member_ids, urls)
125	    }
126	    # Per-source budget of 1 so we deterministically get exactly one unit for the work.
127	    os.environ["PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_SPANS_PER_SOURCE"] = "1"
128	    try:
129	        draft = build_verified_span_draft(member_ids, pool)
130	    finally:
131	        os.environ.pop("PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_SPANS_PER_SOURCE", None)
132	
133	    # The clean sentence must appear exactly ONCE (consolidated to one unit), not 4x.
134	    occurrences = draft.count("Automation displaced roughly 12 percent")
135	    if occurrences != 1:
136	        _fail(
137	            "b",
138	            f"same-work members did NOT consolidate to ONE unit "
139	            f"(clean sentence appears {occurrences}x, expected 1)",
140	        )
141	    # All 4 URLs are retained as grounding corroborator markers on the one unit.
142	    markers = _EV_MARKER_RE.findall(draft)
143	    missing = [m for m in member_ids if m not in markers]
144	    if missing:
145	        _fail(
146	            "b",
147	            f"consolidated unit dropped corroborator URL(s) {missing} "
148	            f"(markers found={markers}) — must KEEP ALL same-work URLs",
149	        )
150	    if len(markers) != 4:
151	        _fail(
152	            "b",
153	            f"expected exactly 4 co-citation markers on the one work, got {len(markers)}: {markers}",
154	        )
155	
156	
157	def _check_b2_over_merge_negative_control() -> None:
158	    """#4 OVER-MERGE NEGATIVE CONTROL (#1289): two records with the SAME folded title
159	    but DIFFERENT year AND different first author (no DOI) are DIFFERENT works and must
160	    NOT consolidate — title-alone never merges (§-1.3: under-merge safe, over-merge
161	    corrupts attribution). Each must surface as its OWN unit with its OWN single marker.
162	    """
163	    # Two distinct works sharing a generic title; distinct year + author, no DOI.
164	    sentence_one = (
165	        "Deep learning models reached 94 percent accuracy on the held-out vision "
166	        "benchmark in the first reported evaluation"
167	    )
168	    sentence_two = (
169	        "Transformer architectures reached 97 percent accuracy on the held-out vision "
170	        "benchmark in the later reported evaluation"
171	    )
172	    pool = {
173	        "ev_d1": {
174	            "evidence_id": "ev_d1",
175	            "direct_quote": f"{sentence_one}.",
176	            "title": "Deep Learning Methods for Computer Vision",
177	            "year": 2019,
178	            "authors": ["Smith J", "Lee K"],
179	            "source_url": "https://venue-a.org/dl",
180	        },
181	        "ev_d2": {
182	            "evidence_id": "ev_d2",
183	            "direct_quote": f"{sentence_two}.",
184	            "title": "deep learning methods for computer vision",
185	            "year": 2023,
186	            "authors": ["Garcia R", "Patel S"],
187	            "source_url": "https://venue-b.net/dl",
188	        },
189	    }
190	    os.environ["PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_SPANS_PER_SOURCE"] = "1"
191	    try:
192	        draft = build_verified_span_draft(["ev_d1", "ev_d2"], pool)
193	    finally:
194	        os.environ.pop("PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_SPANS_PER_SOURCE", None)
195	
196	    markers = _EV_MARKER_RE.findall(draft)
197	    # Both works must surface as DISTINCT units, each carrying ONLY its own marker.
198	    if "ev_d1" not in markers or "ev_d2" not in markers:
199	        _fail(
200	            "b2",
201	            f"a distinct same-title work was lost — both ev_d1 AND ev_d2 must surface "
202	            f"(markers found={markers})",
203	        )
204	    # NOT consolidated => they are NOT co-cited on one unit. Each unit's own marker
205	    # appears exactly once, and the two distinct sentences both render.
206	    if markers.count("ev_d1") != 1 or markers.count("ev_d2") != 1:
207	        _fail(
208	            "b2",
209	            f"same-title-different-discriminator works were WRONGLY co-cited / merged "
210	            f"(markers={markers}); they must stay TWO distinct single-marker units",
211	        )
212	    if "Deep learning models reached 94 percent" not in draft or \
213	            "Transformer architectures reached 97 percent" not in draft:
214	        _fail("b2", "a distinct work's own sentence was dropped (over-merge collapsed it)")
215	
216	
217	def _check_b3_weak_signal_over_merge_negatives() -> None:
218	    """#4 P2 over-merge negatives (#1289): a SINGLE weak shared signal must NOT merge
219	    two distinct same-title works.
220	
221	    (a) SAME title + SAME year + DIFFERENT first-author surname, no DOI -> the year
222	        agrees but the author disagrees, so the strong-path token differs -> NOT merged.
223	        (The OLD first-available rule keyed on year FIRST and would have wrongly merged.)
224	    (b) SAME title + SAME host + DIFFERENT year, no DOI, no strong signal -> the two
225	        weak signals do NOT both agree (year differs), so NOT merged. (host alone is
226	        never enough; year alone is never enough.)
227	    """
228	    # (a) same title + same year (2020) + DIFFERENT author surname.
229	    sent_a1 = (
230	        "Robotic process automation cut routine ledger postings by 31 percent in the "
231	        "first audited rollout across the surveyed firms"
232	    )
233	    sent_a2 = (
234	        "Robotic process automation cut routine ledger postings by 18 percent in the "
235	        "second audited rollout across the surveyed firms"
236	    )
237	    pool_a = {
238	        "ev_y1": {
239	            "evidence_id": "ev_y1",
240	            "direct_quote": f"{sent_a1}.",
241	            "title": "Robotic Process Automation in Financial Operations",
242	            "year": 2020,
243	            "authors": ["Nguyen T", "Park J"],
244	            "source_url": "https://venue-x.org/rpa",
245	        },
246	        "ev_y2": {
247	            "evidence_id": "ev_y2",
248	            "direct_quote": f"{sent_a2}.",
249	            "title": "robotic process automation in financial operations",
250	            "year": 2020,
251	            "authors": ["Ibrahim A", "Costa L"],
252	            "source_url": "https://venue-y.net/rpa",
253	        },
254	    }
255	    os.environ["PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_SPANS_PER_SOURCE"] = "1"
256	    try:
257	        draft_a = build_verified_span_draft(["ev_y1", "ev_y2"], pool_a)
258	    finally:
259	        os.environ.pop("PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_SPANS_PER_SOURCE", None)
260	    markers_a = _EV_MARKER_RE.findall(draft_a)
261	    if markers_a.count("ev_y1") != 1 or markers_a.count("ev_y2") != 1:
262	        _fail(
263	            "b3a",
264	            f"same title + same year + DIFFERENT author was WRONGLY merged/co-cited "
265	            f"(markers={markers_a}); a single matching weak signal (year) must NOT merge "
266	            f"two distinct works",
267	        )
268	    if "31 percent" not in draft_a or "18 percent" not in draft_a:
269	        _fail("b3a", "a distinct same-title work's own sentence was dropped (over-merge)")
270	
271	    # (b) same title + same host + DIFFERENT year, no strong signal.
272	    sent_b1 = (
273	        "Clerical employment in the region contracted by 9 percent during the earlier "
274	        "measurement window of the longitudinal study"
275	    )
276	    sent_b2 = (
277	        "Clerical employment in the region contracted by 14 percent during the later "
278	        "measurement window of the longitudinal study"
279	    )
280	    pool_b = {
281	        "ev_h1": {
282	            "evidence_id": "ev_h1",
283	            "direct_quote": f"{sent_b1}.",
284	            "title": "Clerical Employment Trends in the Mountain Region",
285	            "year": 2018,
286	            "source_url": "https://shared-host.example.org/clerical-2018",
287	        },
288	        "ev_h2": {
289	            "evidence_id": "ev_h2",
290	            "direct_quote": f"{sent_b2}.",
291	            "title": "clerical employment trends in the mountain region",
292	            "year": 2022,
293	            "source_url": "https://shared-host.example.org/clerical-2022",
294	        },
295	    }
296	    os.environ["PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_SPANS_PER_SOURCE"] = "1"
297	    try:
298	        draft_b = build_verified_span_draft(["ev_h1", "ev_h2"], pool_b)
299	    finally:
300	        os.environ.pop("PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_SPANS_PER_SOURCE", None)
301	    markers_b = _EV_MARKER_RE.findall(draft_b)
302	    if markers_b.count("ev_h1") != 1 or markers_b.count("ev_h2") != 1:
303	        _fail(
304	            "b3b",
305	            f"same title + same host + DIFFERENT year was WRONGLY merged/co-cited "
306	            f"(markers={markers_b}); host alone is never a merge key and year differs",
307	        )
308	    if "9 percent" not in draft_b or "14 percent" not in draft_b:
309	        _fail("b3b", "a distinct same-title work's own sentence was dropped (over-merge)")
310	
311	
312	def _check_c_captcha_stub_dropped() -> None:
313	    """#7: a CAPTCHA / security-stub member is dropped; a clean member survives.
314	
315	    I-beatboth-011 #7 P1 (#1289): the drop requires the trigger phrase "just a moment"
316	    AND a strong WAF/security co-token. A bare "just a moment" in otherwise-substantive
317	    prose must NOT be dropped (§-1.3 keep-all).
318	
319	    POSITIVE: "Just a moment... Performing security verification... Cloudflare Ray ID"
320	    carries trigger + co-tokens => DROPPED.
321	    NEGATIVE CONTROL: "Just a moment, the unemployment rate fell to 3.5% in 2023 according
322	    to BLS" carries the bare trigger but NO security co-token => real prose => KEPT.
323	    """
324	    bare_sentence = (
325	        "Just a moment, the unemployment rate fell to 3.5 percent in 2023 according to "
326	        "the Bureau of Labor Statistics surveyed establishment data"
327	    )
328	    pool = {
329	        "ev_captcha": {
330	            "evidence_id": "ev_captcha",
331	            "direct_quote": (
332	                "Just a moment... www.example.org needs to review the security of your "
333	                "connection before proceeding. Performing security verification. "
334	                "Cloudflare Ray ID: abc123."
335	            ),
336	            "doi": "10.9999/captcha",
337	            "title": "Cloudflare interstitial stub page",
338	            "source_url": "https://blocked.example.org",
339	        },
340	        "ev_bare": {
341	            "evidence_id": "ev_bare",
342	            "direct_quote": f"{bare_sentence}.",
343	            "doi": "10.3000/bare",
344	            "title": "Bureau of Labor Statistics employment update",
345	            "source_url": "https://realnews.example.org",
346	        },
347	        "ev_clean": {
348	            "evidence_id": "ev_clean",
349	            "direct_quote": f"{_CLEAN_SENTENCE}.",
350	            "doi": "10.2000/clean",
351	            "title": "A genuine labor-market finding",
352	            "source_url": "https://good.example.org",
353	        },
354	    }
355	    draft = build_verified_span_draft(["ev_captcha", "ev_bare", "ev_clean"], pool)
356	    markers = _EV_MARKER_RE.findall(draft)
357	    if "ev_captcha" in markers:
358	        _fail("c", "CAPTCHA-stub member ev_captcha was NOT dropped (its marker leaked)")
359	    if "security verification" in draft.lower() or "cloudflare ray id" in draft.lower():
360	        _fail("c", "CAPTCHA-stub text leaked into the render")
361	    if "ev_bare" not in markers:
362	        _fail(
363	            "c",
364	            "NEGATIVE CONTROL: bare 'just a moment' real prose (no security co-token) was "
365	            "WRONGLY dropped — §-1.3 keep-all violated",
366	        )
367	    if "unemployment rate fell to 3.5 percent" not in draft:
368	        _fail("c", "NEGATIVE CONTROL: the bare-trigger real sentence was lost (over-drop)")
369	    if "ev_clean" not in markers:
370	        _fail("c", "the clean member was wrongly dropped alongside the CAPTCHA stub")
371	
372	
373	def main() -> int:
374	    _check_a_render_safety()
375	    _check_b_same_work_consolidation()
376	    _check_b2_over_merge_negative_control()
377	    _check_b3_weak_signal_over_merge_negatives()
378	    _check_c_captcha_stub_dropped()
379	
380	    if _FAILURES:
381	        print("HARNESS FAILED — regressions detected:", file=sys.stderr)
382	        for f in _FAILURES:
383	            print(f"  FAIL {f}", file=sys.stderr)
384	        return 1
385	    print("HARNESS PASSED: (a) render-safe (no NUL/C0, blob bounded) "
386	          "(b) same-work consolidated to ONE multi-URL unit "
387	          "(c) CAPTCHA stub dropped")
388	    return 0
389	
390	
391	if __name__ == "__main__":
392	    sys.exit(main())
393

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-beatboth-011 idx49 (#1289) — behavioral replay-harness for the fetch-shell
3	EVIDENCE-PATH leak (§-1.4 fail-loud).
4	
5	THE DEFECT this proves the fix for: ``clean_fetch_body`` returns
6	``CleanedFetch(cleaned_text, shell_reason)``. ``shell_reason`` is set (non-None)
7	when the WHOLE cleaned body is a fetch SHELL (boilerplate / soft-404 / a wholly
8	chrome reference-listing page that cleans to empty). The FRAME-ENTITY path already
9	consumes this signal and routes a shell to its METADATA_ONLY gap branch. But the
10	two EVIDENCE-path consumers in ``live_retriever`` discarded ``shell_reason``
11	(``clean_fetch_body(content).cleaned_text``) and built a cited ``direct_quote`` /
12	evidence row from the junk anyway. On the drb_72 run this leaked junk (e.g. the
13	scirp.org reference-listing page below) into the cited evidence pool.
14	
15	THE FIX consumes the EXISTING ``shell_reason`` signal at BOTH evidence-path sites
16	(``refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics`` ~L2078 and the ``run_live_retrieval``
17	per-URL row builder ~L4561) and routes a shell row to the EXISTING skip/gap branch
18	(NOT a cited evidence row). No new drop/cap/threshold; the existing 1200-char gate
19	is untouched; the faithfulness engine is untouched.
20	
21	This is a BEHAVIORAL end-to-end harness (NOT a flag-set / unit-of-clean_fetch_body
22	check). It drives the REAL consumers and asserts on the EMITTED evidence:
23	  • Case A (EXCLUDED): a wholly-junk shell body is NOT emitted as a cited evidence
24	    row / direct_quote — it is routed to the existing skip/gap branch.
25	  • Case B (KEPT): a real article body IS emitted as a normal evidence row — the
26	    fix never drops a real source (§-1.3 removes only confirmed fetch-junk).
27	
28	Both fixture bodies are ADAPTED from REAL bodies in the drb_72 evidence pool
29	(``outputs/audits/drb_72_2026_06_12/runA_final/evidence_pool.json``) — same source,
30	same shape, lightly trimmed/representative (not byte-verbatim):
31	  • JUNK  = a scirp.org reference-listing page (all markdown link-chrome) that
32	            ``clean_fetch_body`` cleans to empty → ``shell_reason='empty_after_clean'``.
33	            The stored scirp quote in that pool re-cleans to empty under the current
34	            chrome-stripper; this is the genuine fetch-junk the SITE-2 fix removes.
35	  • REAL  = the AEA ``jep.33.2.3`` automation/labor article body → ``shell_reason=None``.
36	
37	SCOPE — what this fix does and does NOT close (honest, per §-1.1):
38	  • Closes the EMPTY-after-clean / boilerplate-dominated error-stub shell class at
39	    BOTH evidence-path sites (e.g. the scirp reference-listing page).
40	  • Does NOT close the longer Cloudflare/CAPTCHA INTERSTITIAL class named in the
41	    issue — the IMF elibrary "solve a puzzle" and BMJ "you have been blocked" bodies
42	    (~500-780 chars) fire neither ``clean_fetch_body.shell_reason`` NOR frame_fetcher's
43	    ``_is_fetch_shell`` and REMAIN cited. Catching that class needs a NEW phrase
44	    detector, which is a NEW drop rule (forbidden here by §-1.3 / the no-new-threshold
45	    constraint) → tracked as a separate follow-up, NOT this PR.
46	  • Site-1 note: for the empty-cleaning scirp fixture, site 1 ALREADY excluded it
47	    pre-fix via the ``thin_content`` length gate; the idx49 fix re-labels that
48	    exclusion ``fetch_shell`` (honest attribution). The genuine NEW exclusion is at
49	    site 2, whose per-URL row builder has no length gate before the append.
50	
51	FAIL-LOUD: any assertion failure exits non-zero. PASS exits 0.
52	
53	Run:  python scripts/iarch_beatboth011_idx49_fetchshell_evidence_harness.py
54	"""
55	from __future__ import annotations
56	
57	import os
58	import sys
59	
60	# Repo root on sys.path so ``src...`` imports resolve when run directly.
61	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
62	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:
63	    sys.path.insert(0, _REPO_ROOT)
64	
65	from src.tools.access_bypass import clean_fetch_body  # noqa: E402
66	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import live_retriever as lr  # noqa: E402
67	
68	
69	# ── Fixtures ADAPTED from real drb_72 evidence-pool bodies (not byte-verbatim) ──
70	#
71	# JUNK: a scirp.org "reference list" page (its ABSTRACT tail trimmed for the
72	# fixture). Every line is markdown link-chrome (TITLE/AUTHORS/KEYWORDS/JOURNAL
73	# NAME all point at ``../journal/...`` slugs). ``strip_web_boilerplate`` strips
74	# the link-chrome and the whole unit cleans to empty → ``clean_fetch_body``
75	# returns ``shell_reason='empty_after_clean'``. On the RAW body it is NOT content-
76	# starved and NOT a landing page, so at SITE 2 (the per-URL row builder, which has
77	# no length gate before the append) NO prior gate excludes it — only the idx49 fix
78	# does. The stored scirp quote in the drb_72 pool re-cleans to empty the same way.
79	_JUNK_SHELL_BODY = (
80	    "TITLE: [ Technological Advances and the Changing Nature of Work: Deriving a "
81	    "Future Skills Set](../journal/paperinformation?paperid=95586) AUTHORS: "
82	    "[Yasmin Danuser](../journal/articles?searchcode=Yasmin+Danuser&searchfield="
83	    "authors&page=1), [Michael J. Kendzia](../journal/articles?searchcode="
84	    "Michael+J.+Kendzia&searchfield=authors&page=1) KEYWORDS: [Destruction Effect]"
85	    "(../journal/articles?searchcode=Destruction+Effect&searchfield=keyword&page=1), "
86	    "[Capitalization Effect](../journal/articles?searchcode=Capitalization+Effect&"
87	    "searchfield=keyword&page=1), [Soft Skills](../journal/articles?searchcode="
88	    "Soft+Skills&searchfield=keyword&page=1) JOURNAL NAME: [Advances in Applied "
89	    "Sociology](../journal/home?journalid=1002), [Vol.9 No.10](../journal/home?"
90	    "issueid=13047), October 10, 2019"
91	)
92	
93	# REAL: representative prose from the AEA Journal of Economic Perspectives
94	# automation/labor article (jep.33.2.3) — its abstract head, lightly extended for
95	# the fixture so it reads as a full body. Long real prose; ``clean_fetch_body``
96	# leaves it intact with ``shell_reason=None``. This row MUST survive the fix as a
97	# cited evidence row (§-1.3: the fix never drops a real source).
98	_REAL_ARTICLE_BODY = (
99	    "We present a framework for understanding the effects of automation and other "
100	    "types of technological changes on labor demand, and use it to interpret "
101	    "changes in US employment over the recent past. At the center of our framework "
102	    "is the allocation of tasks to capital and labor. Automation, which allows "
103	    "capital to replace labor in tasks it was previously engaged in, shifts the "
104	    "task content of production against labor because of a displacement effect. "
105	    "The displacement effect can be offset by a countervailing reinstatement "
106	    "effect that creates new tasks in which labor has a comparative advantage. "
107	    "We argue that the recent stagnation of labor demand is explained by an "
108	    "acceleration of the displacement effect, a weaker reinstatement effect, and "
109	    "slower productivity growth than in previous decades. We document that the "
110	    "real wages of workers without a college degree fell over this period, while "
111	    "the task content of production shifted away from labor in manufacturing and "
112	    "several other sectors of the US economy across the enrolled study period."
113	)
114	
115	_SEED_URL_JUNK = "https://www.scirp.org/reference/referencespapers?referenceid=2601244"
116	_SEED_URL_REAL = "https://www.aeaweb.org/articles/pdf/doi/10.1257/jep.33.2.3"
117	
118	
119	def _fail(msg: str) -> None:
120	    print(f"FAIL: {msg}")
121	    sys.exit(1)
122	
123	
124	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125	# Fixture sanity: confirm clean_fetch_body really classifies our two REAL bodies
126	# the way the rest of the harness depends on. If the detector ever changes such
127	# that the junk no longer reports a shell (or the real body starts to), this
128	# fails LOUD here rather than silently passing a meaningless behavioral check.
129	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
130	def _check_fixture_signals() -> None:
131	    junk_cf = clean_fetch_body(_JUNK_SHELL_BODY)
132	    if not junk_cf.shell_reason:
133	        _fail(
134	            "fixture JUNK body did not report a shell_reason — the harness can no "
135	            f"longer exercise the fix (clean_fetch_body returned shell_reason="
136	            f"{junk_cf.shell_reason!r}, cleaned_len={len(junk_cf.cleaned_text)})"
137	        )
138	    real_cf = clean_fetch_body(_REAL_ARTICLE_BODY)
139	    if real_cf.shell_reason:
140	        _fail(
141	            "fixture REAL article body was wrongly reported as a shell "
142	            f"(shell_reason={real_cf.shell_reason!r}) — a real source would be "
143	            "dropped; the fix must NEVER drop a real source (§-1.3)"
144	        )
145	    print(
146	        f"  fixture signals OK: JUNK shell_reason={junk_cf.shell_reason!r}, "
147	        f"REAL shell_reason={real_cf.shell_reason!r}"
148	    )
149	
150	
151	def _stub_fetch_content_factory(body: str):
152	    """Return a ``_fetch_content`` stub yielding ``body`` for every URL.
153	
154	    Mirrors the real ``_fetch_content`` 5-tuple
155	    ``(content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld)``. ``ok=True`` + a non-empty body
156	    is the NORMAL fetch verdict, so the body flows straight into the per-URL
157	    ``if content:`` row-builder path — exactly where the idx49 guard lives. We do
158	    NOT use the degraded ``ok=False`` path so the ONLY thing that can exclude the
159	    junk row is the new shell guard (not a pre-existing stub/starvation gate).
160	    """
161	
162	    def _stub(url, max_chars, *args, **kwargs):
163	        return (body, True, "Fixture source", "full_text", "")
164	
165	    return _stub
166	
167	
168	def _run_seed_only(monkeypatch_env, *, body: str, seed_url: str) -> list:
169	    """Drive the REAL ``run_live_retrieval`` seed_only loop on one URL whose
170	    fetched body is ``body``. Returns the emitted ``evidence_rows``.
171	
172	    The redesign flag is ON — that is the cert config where the junk leaked, and
173	    it is the ONLY config under which a row reaches the down-weight-and-KEEP
174	    branch (so the idx49 guard must pre-empt it). Offline + cheap: OA resolver
175	    off, OpenAlex enrichment stubbed, serial fetch.
176	    """
177	    saved = {}
178	    for k, v in monkeypatch_env.items():
179	        saved[k] = os.environ.get(k)
180	        os.environ[k] = v
181	
182	    orig_fetch = lr._fetch_content
183	    orig_enrich = getattr(lr, "_bounded_openalex_enrich", None)
184	    try:
185	        lr._fetch_content = _stub_fetch_content_factory(body)
186	        if orig_enrich is not None:
187	            lr._bounded_openalex_enrich = lambda *a, **k: {}
188	        result = lr.run_live_retrieval(
189	            research_question="ai automation labor demand future skills",
190	            seed_urls=[seed_url],
191	            seed_only=True,
192	            enable_openalex_enrich=False,
193	            enable_prefetch_filter=False,
194	            anchor_seed=False,
195	        )
196	        return result.evidence_rows
197	    finally:
198	        lr._fetch_content = orig_fetch
199	        if orig_enrich is not None:
200	            lr._bounded_openalex_enrich = orig_enrich
201	        for k, old in saved.items():
202	            if old is None:
203	                os.environ.pop(k, None)
204	            else:
205	                os.environ[k] = old
206	
207	
208	_ENV = {
209	    # Serial fetch so the stubbed _fetch_content is the seam.
210	    "PG_USE_PARALLEL_FETCH": "0",
211	    # Redesign ON: the cert config where the junk leaked + the only path that
212	    # reaches the down-weight-and-keep branch the guard must pre-empt.
213	    "PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN": "1",
214	    # Keep the loop offline + cheap.
215	    "PG_ENABLE_LIVE_OA_RESOLVER": "0",
216	}
217	
218	
219	def test_site2_junk_shell_excluded_from_cited_evidence() -> None:
220	    """Case A — the wholly-junk reference-listing shell is NOT emitted as a cited
221	    evidence row (routed to the existing skip/gap branch instead)."""
222	    rows = _run_seed_only(_ENV, body=_JUNK_SHELL_BODY, seed_url=_SEED_URL_JUNK)
223	    if rows:
224	        _fail(
225	            "junk fetch-shell was EMITTED as a cited evidence row "
226	            f"(expected 0 rows, got {len(rows)}): "
227	            f"{[r.get('source_url') for r in rows]!r} — the idx49 guard did not "
228	            "fire on the real-output path"
229	        )
230	    print("  [site2] PASS: junk fetch-shell excluded from cited evidence (0 rows)")
231	
232	
233	def test_site2_real_article_still_emitted() -> None:
234	    """Case B — a real article body IS emitted as a normal evidence row with a
235	    populated direct_quote. The fix must never drop a real source."""
236	    rows = _run_seed_only(_ENV, body=_REAL_ARTICLE_BODY, seed_url=_SEED_URL_REAL)
237	    if len(rows) != 1:
238	        _fail(
239	            f"real article was not emitted as exactly one evidence row "
240	            f"(got {len(rows)} rows) — the fix dropped a real source"
241	        )
242	    row = rows[0]
243	    if not row.get("direct_quote") or len(row["direct_quote"]) < 100:
244	        _fail(
245	            "real article evidence row has no usable direct_quote "
246	            f"(len={len(row.get('direct_quote', ''))})"
247	        )
248	    if "displacement effect" not in row["direct_quote"].lower():
249	        _fail("real article direct_quote did not carry the source prose")
250	    print("  [site2] PASS: real article emitted as a normal cited evidence row")
251	
252	
253	def test_site1_junk_shell_not_extractable() -> None:
254	    """Case A for site 1 — ``refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics`` returns an
255	    EMPTY quote + ``failure_mode='fetch_shell'`` for a junk shell body (routed to
256	    the existing not-extractable failure branch, NOT cited)."""
257	    orig_fetch = lr._fetch_content
258	    try:
259	        lr._fetch_content = _stub_fetch_content_factory(_JUNK_SHELL_BODY)
260	        quote, diag = lr.refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics(
261	            "https://www.scirp.org/reference/referencespapers?referenceid=2601244"
262	        )
263	    finally:
264	        lr._fetch_content = orig_fetch
265	    if quote:
266	        _fail(
267	            "site1: junk fetch-shell produced a NON-empty extraction quote "
268	            f"(len={len(quote)}) — it would be cited as evidence"
269	        )
270	    if diag.get("failure_mode") != "fetch_shell":
271	        _fail(
272	            "site1: junk fetch-shell did not record failure_mode='fetch_shell' "
273	            f"(got {diag.get('failure_mode')!r})"
274	        )
275	    print("  [site1] PASS: junk fetch-shell -> empty quote, failure_mode='fetch_shell'")
276	
277	
278	def test_site1_real_article_extractable() -> None:
279	    """Case B for site 1 — a real article body still yields a non-empty extraction
280	    quote (eligible). The fix never drops a real source."""
281	    orig_fetch = lr._fetch_content
282	    try:
283	        lr._fetch_content = _stub_fetch_content_factory(_REAL_ARTICLE_BODY)
284	        quote, diag = lr.refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics(
285	            "https://www.aeaweb.org/articles/pdf/doi/10.1257/jep.33.2.3"
286	        )
287	    finally:
288	        lr._fetch_content = orig_fetch
289	    if not quote or len(quote) < 100:
290	        _fail(
291	            "site1: real article did not yield a usable extraction quote "
292	            f"(len={len(quote)}) — the fix dropped a real source"
293	        )
294	    if diag.get("failure_mode"):
295	        _fail(
296	            "site1: real article recorded a failure_mode "
297	            f"({diag.get('failure_mode')!r}) — it must be eligible"
298	        )
299	    print("  [site1] PASS: real article -> non-empty extraction quote, eligible")
300	
301	
302	def main() -> int:
303	    print("I-beatboth-011 idx49 — fetch-shell evidence-path behavioral harness")
304	    print("Fixtures: REAL drb_72 bodies (scirp.org junk shell + AEA jep.33.2.3 article)")
305	    print()
306	    _check_fixture_signals()
307	    print()
308	    # Site 2 (run_live_retrieval per-URL row builder) — the persisted cited path.
309	    test_site2_junk_shell_excluded_from_cited_evidence()
310	    test_site2_real_article_still_emitted()
311	    # Site 1 (refetch_for_extraction_with_diagnostics) — the extraction quote path.
312	    test_site1_junk_shell_not_extractable()
313	    test_site1_real_article_extractable()
314	    print()
315	    print("ALL CHECKS PASSED — fetch-shell excluded from cited evidence at BOTH "
316	          "sites; real sources still emitted.")
317	    return 0
318	
319	
320	if __name__ == "__main__":
321	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-beatboth-011 (#1289) — fail-loud harness for the junk-SOURCE screen.
3	
4	§-1.4 behavioral acceptance (non-zero exit on regression). The defect: a handful
5	of NON-source pages were cited / listed in the bibliography + per-claim
6	corroboration of a freshly-rendered wide report
7	(outputs/p6_fixed_replay/workforce/drb_72_ai_labor/report.md, 526 cited sources):
8	  (a) a Chegg homework page ("Solved Please read the following paper: David H.
9	      Autor ... | Chegg.com") stood in for a real paper — [521] in the bibliography
10	      AND woven into the body prose; and
11	  (b) a JS-error-shell page ("We're sorry but our[site] doesn't work properly
12	      without JavaScript enabled. Please enable it to continue.") was quoted as a
13	      corroborator across many claims (host = openalex.org, a REAL repository,
14	      whose other rows carry real abstracts that MUST be kept).
15	
16	FIX: a high-precision, faithfulness-NEUTRAL junk-SOURCE screen
17	(``access_bypass.is_junk_source`` = ``is_junk_source_host`` OR
18	``is_error_shell_text``) wired at the single corpus-consumption seam in
19	``run_honest_sweep_r3.py`` (after every retrieval/merge lane AND the fresh/resume
20	reconcile, BEFORE selection / the approval gate). Junk = a homework-help /
21	Q&A-not-source HOST or a fetch-error SHELL body. It drops ONLY junk — never a
22	real journal / repository / gov / news source (§-1.3 keep-all for REAL sources).
23	strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding are untouched.
24	
25	Asserts (each FAILS LOUD with non-zero exit on regression):
26	  (A) PREDICATE PRECISION:
27	        - a Chegg URL source is DROPPED (host signal);
28	        - an error-shell ("doesn't work properly without JavaScript") source is
29	          DROPPED (text signal);
30	        - a real journal / doi.org / pubmed / repository source is KEPT;
31	        - a real news source is KEPT;
32	        - near-miss hosts ("notchegg.com") and real long prose that merely QUOTES
33	          an error phrase are KEPT (no over-screen).
34	  (B) BEHAVIORAL on the REAL banked corpus_snapshot: when the screen is applied to
35	      the actual drb_72 corpus, EVERY Chegg + JS-error-shell row is removed from
36	      retrieval.evidence_rows + classified_sources, while the REAL openalex.org
37	      abstract rows on the SAME host SURVIVE (proves the effect fires in the real
38	      consumption path, not just the predicate — §-1.4).
39	"""
40	from __future__ import annotations
41	
42	import sys
43	from pathlib import Path
44	
45	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
46	sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO))
47	
48	
49	def _fail(msg: str) -> None:
50	    print(f"FAIL I-beatboth-011 junk-source screen: {msg}")
51	    sys.exit(1)
52	
53	
54	# The exact banked junk strings (drb_72 corpus_snapshot, p6_fixed_replay).
55	_CHEGG_URL = (
56	    "https://www.chegg.com/homework-help/questions-and-answers/"
57	    "please-read-following-paper-david-h-autor-still-many-jobs-history-"
58	    "future-workplace-automat-q105413568"
59	)
60	_SHELL_BODY = (
61	    "We're sorry but ourresearch-website-2 doesn't work properly without "
62	    "JavaScript enabled. Please enable it to continue."
63	)
64	# Real openalex bodies on the SAME host as the shell — MUST be kept.
65	_REAL_OPENALEX_BODIES = (
66	    "Don't Fear the Robots: Automatability and Job Satisfaction DetailsLocations "
67	    "Year: 2020 Type: preprint Abstract: We analyze automatability and job "
68	    "satisfaction across occupations using survey microdata, finding "
69	    "heterogeneous effects on worker wellbeing.",
70	    "Job Polarization and Structural Change Year: 2014 Type: preprint Abstract: "
71	    "Job polarization is a widely documented phenomenon in advanced economies "
72	    "driven by routine-biased technical change and offshoring.",
73	)
74	
75	
76	def _check_predicate() -> None:
77	    from src.tools.access_bypass import (
78	        is_error_shell_text,
79	        is_junk_source,
80	        is_junk_source_host,
81	    )
82
