1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-wire-013 (#1327): FAST render-replay VALIDATOR (the short test).
3	
4	Validates the I-wire-011/012/013 render fixes — chrome-as-claim screening, mid-word
5	TRUNCATION suppression, the possible_metric_mismatch contradiction render-gate, and the
6	analytical-DEPTH layer — in MINUTES, by REPLAYING composition + render + screens from a banked
7	post-verification checkpoint and STOPPING before the 4-role/D8 judge. It skips the ~40min
8	generation (reuses the banked verified sentences) and the ~40min D8 (never invoked).
9	
10	WHY a standalone validator and NOT a `--render-only` flag inside run_one_query (advisor, this
11	session): run_one_query's render block (run_honest_sweep_r3.py L11455-11722) reads dozens of
12	fields off a FULLY-POPULATED ``multi`` MultiSectionResult (per-section SectionResult objects,
13	``credibility_analysis.baskets``, ``bibliography``, ``reliability_header``,
14	``dropped_sentences_final`` ...). NO banked artifact carries ``multi``: the post-verification
15	checkpoint (``postverify_checkpoint.json``) is DATA-ONLY (per-sentence kept/dropped accounting,
16	§-1.3), and the existing ``--resume`` infra either re-runs generation (from
17	``corpus_snapshot.json``) or re-runs D8 — neither is the fast render-only path. So a faithful
18	fast validator must RECONSTRUCT the minimal section state from ``verification_details`` and drive
19	the REAL leaf render/screen/composition functions directly.
20	
21	FAITHFULNESS — calls the REAL pipeline functions, never a reimplementation (the constraint). The
22	fixes live in these leaves and this validator imports + calls them, in the SAME order
23	run_one_query assembles report.md (L11668-11707):
24	  - composition: ``key_findings.build_key_findings`` / ``key_findings.build_depth_layer``
25	                 ``abstract_conclusion.build_abstract`` / ``.build_conclusion``
26	                 ``depth_synthesis.synthesize_cross_source_findings`` (bounded; --skip-depth off)
27	  - render+assembly: ``run_honest_sweep_r3.assemble_report_md`` / ``.dedup_identical_paragraphs``
28	  - SCREENS: ``run_honest_sweep_r3._screen_key_findings_chrome`` /
29	             ``._screen_offtopic_chrome_sections`` / ``._strip_dangling_gap_crossref`` /
30	             ``._correct_contradiction_magnitude_range`` / ``._screen_garbled_headers`` /
31	             ``._render_contradicts_block`` (the I-wire-013 possible_metric_mismatch render-gate)
32	  - chrome canary: ``weighted_enrichment.evaluate_render_chrome_canary`` (mode=enforce)
33	  - §-1.1 audit predicates: ``weighted_enrichment.is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable`` /
34	             ``key_findings.is_truncated_fragment`` / ``contradiction_detector.POSSIBLE_METRIC_MISMATCH_MARKER``
35	
36	DATA-AVAILABILITY HONESTY (§-1.1 false-green guard): the optional render inputs
37	(``contradictions.json`` for the contradiction-noise check, ``bibliography.json`` /
38	``evidence_pool.json`` / ``baskets.json`` for the depth-synthesis network pass) are loaded from
39	the SNAPSHOT DIR only — never cross-imported from a DIFFERENT run (split-brain). When an input is
40	ABSENT the check it gates is labeled ``SKIPPED (input absent)``, NOT silently passed as 0 — a
41	missing-sidecar -> ``contradiction_noise=0 -> PASS`` would be the exact false-green this validator
42	exists to catch.
43	
44	LAW VI: every threshold + path + toggle is a CLI arg / PG_* env read.
45	
46	Usage (LOCAL, instant deterministic check — no network):
47	    python scripts/iwire013_fast_render_audit.py \
48	        --snapshot-dir outputs/iwire013_validate_local --skip-depth
49	
50	Usage (with the bounded depth-synthesis pass when baskets+evidence are banked):
51	    python scripts/iwire013_fast_render_audit.py --snapshot-dir <dir>
52	"""
53	
54	from __future__ import annotations
55	
56	import argparse
57	import asyncio
58	import json
59	import os
60	import sys
61	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
62	from pathlib import Path
63	from typing import Any
64	
65	# Repo root on sys.path so ``scripts.*`` / ``src.*`` import exactly as production does.
66	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
67	if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
68	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
69	
70	# The render fixes are default-ON in the cert slate; pin them here so the REAL functions actually
71	# DO their work on the replay (an OFF flag would make a screen a no-op and the validation
72	# meaningless). ``setdefault`` keeps any explicit operator override.
73	os.environ.setdefault("PG_RENDER_CHROME_CANARY", "enforce")   # canary verdict is COMPUTED + enforced
74	os.environ.setdefault("PG_RENDER_CHROME_SCREEN", "1")          # new chrome categories + truncation ON
75	os.environ.setdefault("PG_SWEEP_DEPTH_LAYER", "1")             # build_depth_layer emits the analytical layer
76	os.environ.setdefault("PG_SWEEP_KEY_FINDINGS", "1")            # key-findings block ON
77	os.environ.setdefault("PG_SYNTHESIS_ABSTRACT_CONCLUSION", "1") # abstract/conclusion composition ON
78	os.environ.setdefault("PG_SWEEP_CONTRADICTS_BLOCK", "1")       # contradicts both-sides block (the render-gate)
79	
80	# Default thresholds (LAW VI — overridable by CLI). FAIL the run if any VALIDATED check breaches.
81	_DEFAULT_CHROME_MAX = 5
82	_DEFAULT_TRUNCATION_MAX = 3
83	_DEFAULT_CONTRADICTION_NOISE_MAX = 0
84	_DEFAULT_DEPTH_MIN = 1
85	
86	_POSTVERIFY_CHECKPOINT = "postverify_checkpoint.json"
87	_VERIFICATION_DETAILS = "verification_details.json"
88	
89	
90	@dataclass
91	class _ReplaySection:
92	    """Minimal stand-in for a ``SectionResult``, reconstructed from ``verification_details``.
93	
94	    Carries ONLY the attributes the real render leaves read via ``getattr``
95	    (``build_key_findings`` / ``build_depth_layer`` / ``build_abstract`` / ``build_conclusion``):
96	    ``title`` / ``verified_text`` / ``sentences_verified`` / ``dropped_due_to_failure`` /
97	    ``is_gap_stub``. ``verified_text`` is the section's banked KEPT (strict_verify-passed)
98	    sentences joined verbatim — the exact prose those leaves lift findings from."""
99	
100	    title: str
101	    verified_text: str
102	    sentences_verified: int
103	    dropped_due_to_failure: bool = False
104	    is_gap_stub: bool = False
105	
106	
107	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
108	# Load + reconstruct
109	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
110	def load_verification_details(snapshot_dir: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
111	    """Load the banked per-sentence verification accounting. Prefer the DATA-ONLY
112	    ``postverify_checkpoint.json`` (the canonical fast-replay checkpoint); fall back to the
113	    standalone ``verification_details.json`` sidecar. Fail loud on absence/corruption (LAW II —
114	    never a silent empty corpus)."""
115	    cp = snapshot_dir / _POSTVERIFY_CHECKPOINT
116	    if cp.is_file():
117	        payload = json.loads(cp.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
118	        vd = payload.get("verification_details")
119	        if isinstance(vd, dict) and vd.get("sections"):
120	            return vd
121	    vdp = snapshot_dir / _VERIFICATION_DETAILS
122	    if vdp.is_file():
123	        vd = json.loads(vdp.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
124	        if isinstance(vd, dict) and vd.get("sections"):
125	            return vd
126	    raise FileNotFoundError(
127	        f"no usable verification accounting in {snapshot_dir} "
128	        f"({_POSTVERIFY_CHECKPOINT} / {_VERIFICATION_DETAILS} absent or sectionless)"
129	    )
130	
131	
132	def reconstruct_sections(verif_details: dict[str, Any]) -> list[_ReplaySection]:
133	    """Rebuild the per-section render input from the banked verification accounting. Each kept
134	    sentence already passed strict_verify and carries its inline ``[#ev:...]`` / ``[N]`` token, so
135	    the joined ``verified_text`` is exactly what ``build_key_findings`` / ``build_depth_layer``
136	    lift from. A section with zero kept sentences gets ``sentences_verified=0`` so the leaves skip
137	    it (their gap-stub guard), matching production."""
138	    sections: list[_ReplaySection] = []
139	    for s in verif_details.get("sections", []):
140	        if not isinstance(s, dict):
141	            continue
142	        kept = [k.get("sentence", "") for k in (s.get("kept") or []) if isinstance(k, dict)]
143	        kept = [t for t in kept if t and t.strip()]
144	        sections.append(
145	            _ReplaySection(
146	                title=str(s.get("title") or ""),
147	                verified_text=" ".join(kept),
148	                sentences_verified=len(kept),
149	                dropped_due_to_failure=bool(s.get("dropped_due_to_failure", False)),
150	            )
151	        )
152	    return sections
153	
154	
155	def _section_body_md(sections: list[_ReplaySection]) -> str:
156	    """Reconstruct ``sections_concat`` — the body prose the section-level screens operate on:
157	    one ``### Title`` header + the verbatim verified prose per non-dropped, non-empty section."""
158	    blocks: list[str] = []
159	    for sr in sections:
160	        if sr.dropped_due_to_failure or sr.sentences_verified == 0 or not sr.verified_text.strip():
161	            continue
162	        title = sr.title or "Section"
163	        blocks.append(f"### {title}\n\n{sr.verified_text.strip()}\n")
164	    return "\n\n".join(blocks) + ("\n\n" if blocks else "")
165	
166	
167	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
168	# Optional render inputs (present -> validate; absent -> SKIP, never false-green)
169	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
170	@dataclass
171	class _OptionalInputs:
172	    contradictions_path: str | None = None
173	    bibliography: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
174	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict] = field(default_factory=dict)
175	    baskets: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
176	
177	
178	def load_optional_inputs(snapshot_dir: Path) -> _OptionalInputs:
179	    """Load render inputs that are banked ONLY in a richer snapshot. Each is loaded from THIS
180	    snapshot dir alone (never another run). Absence is fine — it downgrades the gated check to
181	    SKIPPED, never a false pass."""
182	    opt = _OptionalInputs()
183	    cpath = snapshot_dir / "contradictions.json"
184	    if cpath.is_file():
185	        opt.contradictions_path = str(cpath)
186	    bpath = snapshot_dir / "bibliography.json"
187	    if bpath.is_file():
188	        try:
189	            opt.bibliography = json.loads(bpath.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or []
190	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — malformed optional input -> treat as absent
191	            opt.bibliography = []
192	    epath = snapshot_dir / "evidence_pool.json"
193	    if epath.is_file():
194	        try:
195	            rows = json.loads(epath.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or []
196	            opt.evidence_pool = {
197	                r["evidence_id"]: r for r in rows if isinstance(r, dict) and r.get("evidence_id")
198	            }
199	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
200	            opt.evidence_pool = {}
201	    kpath = snapshot_dir / "baskets.json"
202	    if kpath.is_file():
203	        try:
204	            opt.baskets = json.loads(kpath.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or []
205	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
206	            opt.baskets = []
207	    return opt
208	
209	
210	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
211	# Render (mirrors run_one_query L11668-11707 — REAL functions, SAME order)
212	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
213	def assemble_report(
214	    sections: list[_ReplaySection],
215	    opt: _OptionalInputs,
216	    snapshot_dir: Path,
217	    *,
218	    skip_depth: bool,
219	) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
220	    """Drive composition + render + ALL screens and return ``(report_md, n_key_findings_bullets,
221	    n_cross_source_synth)``. Calls the REAL leaves in run_one_query's order."""
222	    import scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 as R
223	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.abstract_conclusion import build_abstract, build_conclusion
224	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.key_findings import build_depth_layer, build_key_findings
225	
226	    question = "I-wire-013 fast render-replay validation"
227	
228	    sections_concat = _section_body_md(sections)
229	
230	    # 1) Key Findings (composition) — first verified sentence per section.
231	    key_findings = build_key_findings(sections)
232	
233	    # 2) Depth: optional bounded cross-source synthesis pass + the per-section analytical layer.
234	    synth_findings = _run_depth_synthesis(sections, opt, skip_depth=skip_depth)
235	    depth_layer = build_depth_layer(sections, synthesized_findings=synth_findings)
236	
237	    # 3) Chrome screen on the Key-Findings bullets (real fix).
238	    key_findings = R._screen_key_findings_chrome(key_findings)
239	
240	    # 4) Off-topic / chrome section screen + dangling-crossref strip on the section body.
241	    placeholder_nums = R._placeholder_bib_nums(opt.bibliography or [])
242	    sections_concat = R._screen_offtopic_chrome_sections(sections_concat, placeholder_nums)
243	    sections_concat = R._strip_dangling_gap_crossref(sections_concat, str(snapshot_dir))
244	
245	    # 5) Contradiction render-gate: the CONTRADICTS both-sides block (skips possible_metric_mismatch
246	    #    predicates, L2464) + the magnitude-range correction. Both read contradictions.json.
247	    methods = ""
248	    if opt.contradictions_path:
249	        methods += R._render_contradicts_block(opt.contradictions_path)
250	        sections_concat = R._correct_contradiction_magnitude_range(
251	            sections_concat, opt.contradictions_path
252	        )
253	
254	    # 6) Abstract (front) + Conclusion (end) composition.
255	    abstract_md = build_abstract(sections)
256	    conclusion_md = build_conclusion(sections)
257	
258	    # 7) Final assembly (body order verbatim from run_one_query L11692) + header-sanity screen.
259	    body = key_findings + sections_concat + depth_layer + methods
260	    final_report = R.assemble_report_md(
261	        f"# Research report: {question}\n\n",
262	        abstract_md,
263	        body,
264	        conclusion_md,
265	        dedup_enabled=True,
266	    )
267	    final_report = R._screen_garbled_headers(final_report)
268	
269	    n_kf = sum(1 for ln in key_findings.split("\n") if ln.lstrip().startswith("- "))
270	    return final_report, n_kf, len(synth_findings)
271	
272	
273	def _run_depth_synthesis(
274	    sections: list[_ReplaySection], opt: _OptionalInputs, *, skip_depth: bool
275	) -> list[str]:
276	    """The bounded cross-source synthesis pass (depth_synthesis.synthesize_cross_source_findings).
277	    Runs ONLY when ``--skip-depth`` is off AND the inputs it needs (baskets + evidence_pool) are
278	    banked in the snapshot. Otherwise returns ``[]`` (the per-section depth layer still renders, so
279	    depth_grounded_findings stays >=1 from key_findings). Fail-open: a synthesis error never aborts
280	    the validator (it is additive)."""
281	    if skip_depth or not opt.baskets or not opt.evidence_pool:
282	        return []
283	    try:
284	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.depth_synthesis import (
285	            bib_num_by_evidence_id,
286	            depth_synthesis_pre_pass,
287	            make_depth_synthesizer,
288	            synthesize_cross_source_findings,
289	        )
290	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import strict_verify
291	
292	        async def _go() -> list[str]:
293	            precomputed = await depth_synthesis_pre_pass(opt.baskets, opt.evidence_pool)
294	            return synthesize_cross_source_findings(
295	                opt.baskets,
296	                opt.evidence_pool,
297	                synthesizer=make_depth_synthesizer(precomputed),
298	                verify_fn=strict_verify,
299	                bib_num_by_evidence_id=bib_num_by_evidence_id(opt.bibliography or []),
300	            )
301	
302	        return list(asyncio.run(_go()) or [])
303	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive synthesis; never abort the validator
304	        print(f"[depth-synthesis] skipped (fail-open): {exc}")
305	        return []
306	
307	
308	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
309	# §-1.1 audit (REAL predicates) on the EMITTED report.md
310	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
311	def audit_report(
312	    report_md: str,
313	    opt: _OptionalInputs,
314	    n_key_findings: int,
315	    n_cross_source: int,
316	    *,
317	    cross_source_exercised: bool,
318	) -> dict[str, Any]:
319	    """Compute the four §-1.1 render-integrity metrics with the SAME predicates the pipeline uses,
320	    plus a per-check VALIDATED/SKIPPED label."""
321	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.key_findings import is_truncated_fragment
322	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (
323	        _report_claim_bullets,
324	        evaluate_render_chrome_canary,
325	    )
326	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.contradiction_detector import (
327	        POSSIBLE_METRIC_MISMATCH_MARKER,
328	    )
329	
330	    # (a) chrome-as-claim — the canary's own count (NOT a parallel reimplementation).
331	    canary = evaluate_render_chrome_canary(report_md)
332	    chrome_count = int(canary["chrome_claim_bullets"])
333	
334	    # (b) truncation — claim bullets carrying an unambiguous mid-word / cut-span marker.
335	    bullets = _report_claim_bullets(report_md)
336	    truncation_count = sum(1 for b in bullets if is_truncated_fragment(b))
337	
338	    # (c) contradiction NOISE — a possible_metric_mismatch rendered INSIDE the CONTRADICTS headline
339	    #     both-sides block (where it would assert an UNCONFIRMED mismatch as a settled contradiction,
340	    #     the lethal §-1.1 case the I-wire-013 render-gate at _render_contradicts_block:L2464 removes).
341	    #     SCOPED to ``- CONTRADICTS:`` lines deliberately: the SAME marker rendered in the numeric /
342	    #     qualitative DISCLOSURE block is a CORRECT §-1.3 disclosure (kept, never dropped) — counting
343	    #     whole-report markers would false-flag those legitimate disclosures. VALIDATED only when
344	    #     contradictions.json is present (else no CONTRADICTS block is rendered and a 0 proves nothing).
345	    contradiction_validated = bool(opt.contradictions_path)
346	    contradiction_noise = sum(
347	        line.count(POSSIBLE_METRIC_MISMATCH_MARKER)
348	        for line in report_md.split("\n")
349	        if line.lstrip().startswith("- CONTRADICTS:")
350	    )
351	
352	    # (d) depth — grounded cross-source synthesis findings + key-findings bullets. The cross-source
353	    #     leg is EXERCISED only when the depth-synthesis pass actually ran (baskets + evidence banked
354	    #     AND --skip-depth off). Baskets are persisted to NO banked artifact in-tree, so on a standard
355	    #     checkpoint the cross-source fix is STRUCTURALLY unvalidatable by this short test — only the
356	    #     key-findings depth leg is. Surfaced explicitly so a depth PASS carried purely by key_findings
357	    #     is never mistaken for validation of the cross-source synthesis fix.
358	    depth_grounded = n_cross_source + n_key_findings
359	
360	    return {
361	        "chrome": {
362	            "count": chrome_count,
363	            "total_claim_bullets": int(canary["total_claim_bullets"]),
364	            "rate": canary["chrome_as_claim_rate"],
365	            "canary_verdict": canary["verdict"],
366	            "validated": True,
367	        },
368	        "truncation": {"count": truncation_count, "validated": True},
369	        "contradiction": {"count": contradiction_noise, "validated": contradiction_validated},
370	        "depth": {
371	            "count": depth_grounded,
372	            "cross_source": n_cross_source,
373	            "key_findings": n_key_findings,
374	            "validated": True,
375	            "cross_source_exercised": cross_source_exercised,
376	        },
377	    }
378	
379	
380	def evaluate_thresholds(audit: dict[str, Any], args: argparse.Namespace) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
381	    """Apply the FAIL-LOUD thresholds. A check only forces FAIL when it was VALIDATED. SKIPPED
382	    checks are surfaced but never silently pass as a green — they make the overall verdict
383	    ``PASS (partial)``."""
384	    failures: list[str] = []
385	    if audit["chrome"]["count"] > args.chrome_max:
386	        failures.append(f"chrome_as_claim={audit['chrome']['count']} > {args.chrome_max}")
387	    if audit["truncation"]["count"] > args.truncation_max:
388	        failures.append(f"truncation={audit['truncation']['count']} > {args.truncation_max}")
389	    if audit["depth"]["count"] < args.depth_min:
390	        failures.append(f"depth_grounded_findings={audit['depth']['count']} < {args.depth_min}")
391	    if audit["contradiction"]["validated"] and (
392	        audit["contradiction"]["count"] > args.contradiction_noise_max
393	    ):
394	        failures.append(
395	            f"contradiction_noise={audit['contradiction']['count']} > {args.contradiction_noise_max}"
396	        )
397	    return (not failures), failures
398	
399	
400	def _print_table(
401	    audit: dict[str, Any], failures: list[str], skipped: list[str], args: argparse.Namespace
402	) -> None:
403	    def verdict(ok: bool, validated: bool) -> str:
404	        if not validated:
405	            return "SKIPPED (input absent)"
406	        return "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
407	
408	    print("\n=== I-wire-013 fast render-replay sec-1.1 audit ===")
409	    print(f"  (a) chrome-as-claim    : {audit['chrome']['count']:>4}  "
410	          f"({audit['chrome']['count']}/{audit['chrome']['total_claim_bullets']} bullets, "
411	          f"rate={audit['chrome']['rate']}, canary={audit['chrome']['canary_verdict']})  "
412	          f"-> {verdict(audit['chrome']['count'] <= args.chrome_max, True)}")
413	    print(f"  (b) truncation         : {audit['truncation']['count']:>4}  "
414	          f"-> {verdict(audit['truncation']['count'] <= args.truncation_max, True)}")
415	    cval = audit["contradiction"]["validated"]
416	    print(f"  (c) contradiction_noise: {audit['contradiction']['count']:>4}  "
417	          f"(possible_metric_mismatch in '- CONTRADICTS:' lines)  "
418	          f"-> {verdict(audit['contradiction']['count'] <= args.contradiction_noise_max, cval)}")
419	    xs = "exercised" if audit["depth"]["cross_source_exercised"] else "NOT exercised (baskets unbanked)"
420	    print(f"  (d) depth_grounded     : {audit['depth']['count']:>4}  "
421	          f"(cross_source={audit['depth']['cross_source']} [{xs}] + key_findings={audit['depth']['key_findings']})  "
422	          f"-> {verdict(audit['depth']['count'] >= args.depth_min, True)}")
423	    if skipped:
424	        print(f"  NOT EXERCISED on this snapshot: {'; '.join(skipped)}")
425	    if failures:
426	        print(f"  FAILURES: {'; '.join(failures)}")
427	
428	
429	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
430	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="I-wire-013 fast render-replay validator + audit")
431	    parser.add_argument("--snapshot-dir", type=Path, default=Path("outputs/iwire013_validate_local"),
432	                        help="dir holding postverify_checkpoint.json (+ optional sidecars)")
433	    parser.add_argument("--out", type=Path, default=None,
434	                        help="where to write the replayed report.md (default: <snapshot-dir>/iwire013_replay_report.md)")
435	    parser.add_argument("--skip-depth", action="store_true",
436	                        help="skip the bounded depth-synthesis network pass (pure-deterministic, instant)")
437	    parser.add_argument("--chrome-max", type=int, default=_DEFAULT_CHROME_MAX)
438	    parser.add_argument("--truncation-max", type=int, default=_DEFAULT_TRUNCATION_MAX)
439	    parser.add_argument("--contradiction-noise-max", type=int, default=_DEFAULT_CONTRADICTION_NOISE_MAX)
440	    parser.add_argument("--depth-min", type=int, default=_DEFAULT_DEPTH_MIN)
441	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
442	
443	    snapshot_dir = args.snapshot_dir.resolve()
444	    out_path = (args.out or (snapshot_dir / "iwire013_replay_report.md")).resolve()
445	
446	    print(f"[validate] snapshot_dir={snapshot_dir}")
447	    verif_details = load_verification_details(snapshot_dir)
448	    sections = reconstruct_sections(verif_details)
449	    opt = load_optional_inputs(snapshot_dir)
450	    print(f"[validate] sections={len(sections)} "
451	          f"(verified={sum(1 for s in sections if s.sentences_verified)}); "
452	          f"contradictions.json={'yes' if opt.contradictions_path else 'NO'}; "
453	          f"bibliography={len(opt.bibliography)}; baskets={len(opt.baskets)}; skip_depth={args.skip_depth}")
454	
455	    cross_source_exercised = bool(not args.skip_depth and opt.baskets and opt.evidence_pool)
456	    report_md, n_kf, n_synth = assemble_report(sections, opt, snapshot_dir, skip_depth=args.skip_depth)
457	    out_path.write_text(report_md, encoding="utf-8")
458	    print(f"[validate] wrote {out_path} ({len(report_md)} bytes)")
459	
460	    audit = audit_report(
461	        report_md, opt, n_kf, n_synth, cross_source_exercised=cross_source_exercised
462	    )
463	    # Loudly enumerate the headline I-wire-013 checks NOT exercised on this snapshot (inputs absent)
464	    # so a partial PASS is never over-trusted (operator reads by ear).
465	    not_exercised: list[str] = []
466	    if not audit["contradiction"]["validated"]:
467	        not_exercised.append("contradiction render-gate (no contradictions.json for this run_id)")
468	    if not audit["depth"]["cross_source_exercised"]:
469	        not_exercised.append("cross-source depth synthesis (baskets persisted to no artifact)")
470	
471	    ok, failures = evaluate_thresholds(audit, args)
472	    _print_table(audit, failures, not_exercised, args)
473	
474	    if not ok:
475	        print("\n[validate] OVERALL: FAIL")
476	        return 1
477	    if not_exercised:
478	        print(
479	            "\n[validate] OVERALL: PASS (PARTIAL) - validated chrome + truncation + key-findings depth "
480	            "ONLY. NOT exercised on this snapshot: " + "; ".join(not_exercised)
481	            + ". These require a richer checkpoint (contradictions.json + persisted baskets) or a full run."
482	        )
483	        return 0
484	    print("\n[validate] OVERALL: PASS (all four checks validated)")
485	    return 0
486	
487	
488	if __name__ == "__main__":
489	    raise SystemExit(main())
490
